messages.content:
HOW YOUR CLAIM BECOMES A NUMBER (the perturbation contract):
- You never state € impacts. You emit machine-readable ops; the engine computes
  ΔTAM/ΔSAM/ΔYAM by re-running the real funnel. Any € figure you write in prose
  will be stripped.
- Ops: {nodeId, op: "set"|"scale"|"add", value} rewrite one leaf's value;
  {nodeId, op: "exclude"} removes a dimension cell from the venture's reachable
  scope ("unavailable to us" — foreclosure), which is different from shrinking it.
- Ops apply in array order. Ratio nodes must stay inside [0,1]; tamBase must stay > 0.
- Only FUNNEL-ACTIVE nodes may be perturbed (marked in the ledger you receive).
  shape.cagr and shape.cr3 are context — a demand-pace or concentration risk must
  be translated into the funnel nodes it would actually move (tamBase level,
  serviceableFactor reach, obtainableFactor pace, or a share).
- Sibling shares are NOT re-normalized when you move one share (documented v1
  simplification). If you mean a mix-SHIFT, perturb multiple siblings; if you
  mean "this slice is smaller in absolute terms", one op is correct.
- Perturbing an assumption leaf overrides whatever the user set: your claim is
  about the world, not about their slider.

Fix the hypothesis's targetNodes/perturbation so it passes validation WITHOUT changing the claim's meaning. If the claim cannot be expressed against funnel-active nodes, translate it into the funnel nodes it would actually move.

FUNNEL-ACTIVE NODES:
tamBase (EUR_M, value 300)
geo.DE (ratio, value 0.5)
geo.NL (ratio, value 0.28)
geo.PL (ratio, value 0.07)
geo.CH (ratio, value 0.08)
geo.CZ (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.AT (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.other (ratio, value 0.03)
seg.hyperscale (ratio, value 0.44)
seg.colocation (ratio, value 0.18)
seg.enterprise (ratio, value 0.29)
seg.edge (ratio, value 0.05)
seg.telecom (ratio, value 0.04)
cust.operator-large (ratio, value 0.4)
cust.operator-mid (ratio, value 0.25)
cust.oem (ratio, value 0.22)
cust.distributor (ratio, value 0.13)
serviceableFactor (ratio, value 0.55)
obtainableFactor (ratio, value 0.01)

HYPOTHESIS:
{
 "id": "europe-thirty-percent-arithmetic-ghost",
 "title": "A hostile reviewer strikes the unsourced 'Europe ≈ 30%' hop and the €300M loses its floor, not just its band",
 "narrative": "The tamBase derivation looks disciplined — a refinement cycle even killed a contaminated €495M ceiling. But its load-bearing hop, 'Europe ≈ 30% of global', is an inserted analyst estimate: Grand View publishes only North America's 38% and no source in any search pass gives a Europe or CE rack-PDU figure. Worse, the global anchor itself is unsettled — same-category estimates run from $1.697B to $5.3B (QY Research $3.255B and Strategic Market Research $5.3B sit in this venture's own digest), a spread wider than the model's ±20% band. A diligence partner strikes the 30%, pairs the lowest credible global anchor with a conservative Europe share, and lands near €200M — below the band's floor — and the model has no sourced fact with which to push back. The band communicates 'we've bounded the uncertainty'; the derivation chain shows the uncertainty was never bounded, only anchored to one publisher's number times an invented ratio.",
 "category": "fact",
 "targetNodes": [
  "tamBase"
 ],
 "mechanism": [
  {
   "step": "trigger",
   "text": "Diligence cross-references the global anchor: QY Research puts the global data-center PDU market at $3,255M while Strategic Market Research says $5.3B for rack PDUs alone and other publishers go as low as $1.7B — the primary anchor is one draw from a >2x distribution",
   "refs": [
    "digest:cust-mix#c9",
    "digest:cust-mix#c11",
    "ledger:tamBase"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "propagation",
   "text": "With the anchor contested, the unsourced 'Europe ≈ 30%' multiplier and the Statista-based '39.8% of Europe colo MW' CE-7 step (itself scope-inflated vs CBRE's 4,726MW top-15 Europe figure) cannot rescue the chain — every hop is either single-source or inserted",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:tamBase",
    "digest:geo-region-denominator#c5",
    "digest:geo-region-denominator#c0"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "model-impact",
   "text": "tamBase's effective uncertainty is wider and skewed below the stated 240–360 band; every downstream number inherits a base that cannot survive its first hostile audit, which matters most when the model is used to raise capital or justify CE manufacturing capacity",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:tamBase",
    "ledger:serviceableFactor"
   ]
  }
 ],
 "whyMissable": "The refinement-cycle narrative makes the base look stress-tested — a contaminated ceiling was found and removed, which reads as rigor. But the refinement fixed the cross-check while leaving the primary chain's weakest hop (the inserted Europe share) untouched, and even the comparator used to lower the ceiling was itself drawn from a broader-category report. The audit trail creates confidence the audit never earned.",
 "expectedObviousness": "medium",
 "falsifier": "The commissioned IndexBox EU rack-PDU country tables (the ledger's own named promotion path) placing the CE-7 base at €270M or above.",
 "likelihood": {
  "value": 0.4,
  "rationale": "The 2.3x global anchor spread and the unsourced Europe hop are documented facts; whether the true value sits below the band floor (vs merely being unverifiable) is a judgment call — the Germany-alone $509.6M broader-PDU figure cuts the other way, so the distribution is wide in both directions but the defensibility failure is certain.",
  "basis": "judgment"
 },
 "perturbation": [
  {
   "nodeId": "tamBase",
   "op": "set",
   "value": 210,
   "note": "Low-credible-anchor reconstruction: bottom-quartile global rack-PDU estimate × conservative Europe share × unchanged CE-7 step — the number a hostile reviewer writes in the margin"
  }
 ],
 "indicators": [
  {
   "signal": "IndexBox EU rack-PDU report headline (currently paywalled) or any second Europe-scoped rack-PDU figure",
   "where": "IndexBox price list / report catalog; Omdia and Dell'Oro rack-power publication schedules",
   "threshold": "Any directly-scoped EU figure implying CE-7 <€250M",
   "updates": "increases"
  },
  {
   "signal": "Convergence or further divergence of next-vintage global rack-PDU estimates",
   "where": "Grand View, Mordor, QY Research 2026 report refreshes",
   "threshold": "Next-vintage spread across publishers remaining >1.8x",
   "updates": "increases"
  }
 ],
 "mitigations": [
  {
   "action": "Commission the IndexBox EU rack-PDU country tables now — the ledger's own promotion path — before the model is shown to any external audience",
   "type": "information",
   "voiNodeId": "tamBase",
   "note": "Collapses the widest structural uncertainty in the model for the price of one report"
  },
  {
   "action": "Build a bottom-up cross-check: CE-7 installed MW × racks/MW × PDUs/rack × ASP, so the top-down chain has an independent second method rather than a contaminated comparator",
   "type": "operational",
   "voiNodeId": null,
   "note": "A 2-method triangulation survives diligence even if each method is imprecise"
  }
 ],
 "lens": "ledger-self-audit",
 "riskId": "risk.ledger-self-audit.europe-thirty-percent-arithmetic-ghost"
}

VALIDATION ERROR:
[
  {
    "code": "invalid_value",
    "values": [
      "error",
      "risk"
    ],
    "path": [
      0,
      "resolution"
    ],
    "message": "Invalid option: expected one of \"error\"|\"risk\""
  }
]

Emit the corrected hypothesis JSON (same shape).

Omdia and Dell'Oro rack-power publication schedules","threshold":"Any directly-scoped EU figure implying CE-7 <€250M","updates":"increases"},{"signal":"Convergence or further divergence of next-vintage global rack-PDU estimates","where":"Grand View, Mordor, QY Research 2026 report refreshes","threshold":"Next-vintage spread across publishers remaining >1.8x","updates":"increases"}],"mitigations":[{"action":"Commission the IndexBox EU rack-PDU country tables now — the ledger's own promotion path — before the model is shown to any external audience","type":"information","voiNodeId":"tamBase","note":"Collapses the widest structural uncertainty in the model for the price of one report"},{"action":"Build a bottom-up cross-check: CE-7 installed MW × racks/MW × PDUs/rack × ASP, so the top-down chain has an independent second method rather than a contaminated comparator","type":"operational","voiNodeId":null,"note":"A 2-method triangulation survives diligence even if each method is imprecise"}]}
